name: DemoIccMAX
description: A project for handling ICC profiles and their transformations.

dependencies.name: libxml2
dependencies.purpose: Provides XML parsing and serialization for the IccXML2 library and tools.
dependencies.usage: Handles ICC profile data in XML format, enabling efficient transformation and validation.

visual_studio.instructions: Ensure Vcpkg is configured for Visual Studio and install via the integrated Vcpkg tooling.

dependencies.name: libtiff
dependencies.purpose: Supports TIFF image manipulation for image processing tools.
dependencies.usage: Processes ICC profiles embedded in TIFF images and extracts color profile metadata.

visual_studio.instructions: Ensure Vcpkg is set up with Visual Studio for Windows builds.

dependencies.name: wxWidgets
dependencies.purpose: Cross-platform GUI framework for the basic profile viewer.
dependencies.usage: Renders the graphical interface for interacting with ICC profiles.

visual_studio.instructions: Install wxWidgets via Vcpkg in Visual Studio or use wxWidgets source from its official website.

dependencies.name: nlohmann-json
dependencies.purpose: C++ library for JSON parsing and serialization.
dependencies.usage: Handles JSON-based configuration and data interchange, enabling serialization and deserialization of ICC profile metadata.

installation.alternative_installation: Copy json.hpp directly from the repository into your project.

vcpkg.description: Install all dependencies using Vcpkg by running the following command:
vcpkg.notes: Ensure that Vcpkg is configured with your environment. For Visual Studio, follow Microsoft's official documentation to integrate Vcpkg with Visual Studio.

classic.description: Manually install dependencies using package managers like apt, brew, or source downloads.

platforms.debian: Use apt to install each dependency as specified in the 'installation' field.
platforms.macos: Use brew to install each dependency as specified in the 'installation' field.

visual_studio.description: Install dependencies via Vcpkg using Visual Studio's integrated tools.
steps: Ensure that Vcpkg is integrated with Visual Studio (check Tools > Options > Vcpkg).
Run 'vcpkg integrate install' if not already set up.
Install dependencies via the Vcpkg Console or directly in your project settings.
